title: Pocket Flock
description: A cozy mobile game about birdwatching made in Unity with team Birdbrain for Buffalo Buffalo.
thingsLearned: Working in a multidisciplinary team, working with Unity to build a mobile game, developing a game from concept to polished prototype.
approach: I was the development lead for the project, so I designed most of the systems, while also contributing to the game design. We quickly iterated in an agile manner, while keeping a strict deadline for the prototype.

title: Useless Vending Machine
description: A game made in Unity for the Big Mode Game Jam 2026, solo project.
thingsLearned: I participated on two game jams on two consecutive weekends. From this project I learned a lot about scope and time management, since I wanted to make game that was memorable. I focused on audio design to make it a fun and short experience.
approach: I started by scoping my time and designing the core experience. I went with a smaller experience so that it could be the most polished possible within the 3 days that I had. I made all the audio with my mouth, which was a fun challenge, while also making all the mechanics work with minimal bugs.

title: Neil-A's Big Halloween
description: A Unity story driven game made in a team of 7 for the Global Game Jam 2026.
thingsLearned: Working with a team that I just met and making a game in 3 days. We used Yarn, a dialogue system for Unity, which was a great fit for the game, since we had two writers, ending up with a lot of dialogue for a short game.
approach: We started brainstorming around the theme of Mask, while also keeping our team's skills in mind. We ended up with a more narrative experience with a lot of dialogue, so after the main mechanics were done on the second day, we spent the rest of the time adding the dialogue to the game and polishing it.

title: A Disadvantageous Adventure
description: An incremental game made in Unity, solo project.
thingsLearned: Scoping for personal projects while also studying full time. Make games work for mobile devices with save states.
approach: I started with the need to test developing a mobile game, and when I scoped the project for 2 weeks I decided on this small experience with small interactions. Having the Dice roll in a more natural way was a big focus, since the upgrades need to work correctly with it.

title: Final Stand
description: A Blackjack versus AI game made in Unreal, solo project.
thingsLearned: Implementing C++ in Unreal. UI and Bluprint development. Adding animations with simple timelines to make actions feel more engaging.
approach: This game was made in 3 weeks for a course, in which I had to scope and decide which features to focus on. I focused more on the C++ logic to better understand how it works together in Blueprints.
